methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Xaira Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Occam is a Seed-stage biotech company founded by Ana Montero Horas and Andre Marques-Smith, having raised $14M to date. The valuation universe includes 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage comparables in the Biotech & Health sector, though narrow peer filtering yielded insufficient datapoints for reliable median benchmarking.

Only one valuation method proved applicable: Stage-Progression analysis. Using Occam's $14M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement in biotech yields an implied valuation of $35M. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be applied due to the absence of revenue data, which is standard for private companies at this stage. Peer-Median analysis was not executable given insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum cohort size (need ≥3; found only 2 narrow matches across 14 total sector peers).

The analysis supports a valuation range of $30M to $40M, with $35M as the median point. This range reflects limited triangulation—derived from a single applicable method rather than converging multiples. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue traction data, which would enable public-comp anchoring, and the sparse peer set, which limits median benchmarking. Any material update to financing or revenue disclosure would warrant reassessment.
